item.title: Joe Rogan Experience #2307 - Tim Dillon
item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:
Tim Dillon is a stand-up comic, actor, and host of "The Tim Dillon Show" podcast. Look for his new comedy special, "Tim Dillon: I'm Your Mother," now streaming on Netflix. https://www.netflix.com/title/81992010
https://www.timdilloncomedy.com

item.title: Stephen Law - How Do Belief Systems Work?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
Why are ‘belief systems’ so powerful? How do they control religion and politics and all ways of thinking from a scientific worldview to conspiracy theories? What is a ‘belief system’? It is a set of tenets or convictions that support each other, such that the whole system governs individual beliefs. How we believe may be more important than what we believe.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Stephen Law FRSA is an English philosopher. He is currently Director of the Certificate of Higher Education and Director of Philosophy at The Department of Continuing Education, University of Oxford.
Shop the Closer To Truth merch store for unique items like notebooks, mugs, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Michael Graziano - Consciousness and Spirituality
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Like Closer To Truth on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Spirituality requires consciousness because we need to be aware, and to be self-aware, in order to apprehend spirituality of any kind. But is there more to the relationship? Some make the extravagant claim that our human consciousness is somehow connected directly to a’ cosmic consciousness’? Can this make sense? If so, what would it mean?
Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Michael Steven Anthony Graziano is an American scientist and novelist who is currently a professor of psychology and neuroscience at Princeton University. His scientific research focuses on the brain basis of awareness.
Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Deepak Chopra - Why Philosophy of Science?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for new videos, announcements, and more: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/
Science is humankind’s magnificent achievement, the way of thinking to discern facts and truths and to reject errors and myths. But how to understand the scientific method itself and what is it really that science is learning? That’s the role of philosophy. For example, when science discerns ‘regularities’ in nature, are these ‘laws’ of nature?
Register for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.
Deepak Chopra is an Indian-American author, new age guru, and alternative medicine advocate. A prominent figure in the New Age movement, his books and videos have made him one of the best-known and wealthiest figures in alternative medicine.
Contribute what you can to support the show and help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Could consciousness be fundamental?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded: Are the answers in our search to explain consciousness located in the quantum realm? Stuart Hameroff explores the physics of consciousness. #shorts

item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:

item.title: Keith Ward - Can Many Religions All Be True?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase, like a logo hoodie or coffee mug: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Conflicting religions fill the world. Doctrines about God contradict one another. No one denies the diversity of religions and the apparent incompatibility of their core beliefs. Many believe only their own religion to be the truth. Some claim all religions reflect the same truth. Others assert that differing dogmas expose the emptiness of all religion.
Like Closer To Truth on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Keith Ward FBA is an English philosopher and theologian. He is a fellow of the British Academy and a priest of the Church of England. He was a canon of Christ Church, Oxford, until 2003. Comparative theology and the relationship between science and religion are two of his main topics of interest.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Kevin Timpe - How Can Free Will Work?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Assume that free will is real, not an illusion, and that the only reality is physical. How then could the will possibly be free? By what mechanism could human choice transcend the strong determinism of a closed physical world? One can try diminishing one side or the other: reducing free will or softening determinism, but each has its own difficulties.
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
Kevin Timpe currently holds the William H. Jellema Chair in Christian Philosophy at Calvin College. He's been a visiting faculty or scholar at Calvin Theological Seminary, Innsbruck University, Oxford University, and Peking University. He has written or edited ten books and published nearly 50 articles.
Shop the Closer To Truth merch store for unique items like notebooks, mugs, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

item.title: Robert Rodriguez: Sin City, Desperado, El Mariachi, Alita, and Filmmaking | Lex Fridman Podcast #465
item.author: Lex Fridman
item.content:encoded:
Robert Rodriguez is a legendary filmmaker and creator of Sin City, El Mariachi, Desperado, Spy Kids, Machete, From Dusk Till Dawn, Alita: Battle Angel, The Faculty, and his newest venture Brass Knuckle Films. Thank you for listening ❤ Check out our sponsors: https://lexfridman.com/sponsors/ep465-sb See below for timestamps, transcript, and to give feedback, submit questions, contact Lex, etc.
*Transcript:* https://lexfridman.com/robert-rodriguez-transcript
*CONTACT LEX:* *Feedback* - give feedback to Lex: https://lexfridman.com/survey *AMA* - submit questions, videos or call-in: https://lexfridman.com/ama *Hiring* - join our team: https://lexfridman.com/hiring *Other* - other ways to get in touch: https://lexfridman.com/contact
*EPISODE LINKS:* Robert's X: https://x.com/rodriguez Robert's Instagram: https://instagram.com/rodriguez/ Brass Knuckle Films: https://brassknucklefilms.com/ Rebel without a Crew (book): https://amzn.to/3G7gtQJ Rebel without a Crew (audiobook): https://amzn.to/3Ri5wyc
*SPONSORS:* To support this podcast, check out our sponsors & get discounts: *Invideo AI:* AI video generator. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/invideoai-ep465-sb *Brain.fm:* Music for focus. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/brainfm-ep465-sb *NetSuite:* Business management software. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/netsuite-ep465-sb *Shopify:* Sell stuff online. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/shopify-ep465-sb *LMNT:* Zero-sugar electrolyte drink mix. Go to https://lexfridman.com/s/lmnt-ep465-sb
*OUTLINE:* 0:00 - Episode highlight 2:07 - Introduction 4:06 - Explosions and having only one take 11:40 - Success and failure 20:30 - Filmmaking on a low budget 32:43 - El Mariachi 44:12 - Creativity 1:06:07 - Limitations 1:12:24 - Handling criticism 1:28:33 - Action films 1:39:55 - Quentin Tarantino 1:49:54 - Desperado 1:50:56 - Salma Hayek 1:55:42 - Danny Trejo 2:00:56 - Filming in Austin 2:07:07 - Editing 2:16:37 - Sound design 2:21:45 - Deadlines 2:25:16 - Alita: Battle Angel 2:33:38 - James Cameron 2:46:41 - Sin City 3:00:50 - Manifesting 3:12:14 - Memories and journaling 3:21:57 - Mortality
*PODCAST LINKS:* - Podcast Website: https://lexfridman.com/podcast - Apple Podcasts: https://apple.co/2lwqZIr - Spotify: https://spoti.fi/2nEwCF8 - RSS: https://lexfridman.com/feed/podcast/ - Podcast Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLrAXtmErZgOdP_8GztsuKi9nrraNbKKp4 - Clips Channel: https://www.youtube.com/lexclips

item.title: Alan Leshner - Are Science & Religion at War?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://bsky.app/profile/closertotruth.bsky.social
What is it about science and theology that grips people so emotionally, as during a war, such that each is committed to his or her viewpoint (which often are sharply opposed to one another)? Each is fighting for its predominance, if not for its life: religion from being marginalized, science from being politically contained.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Alan Leshner is a psychologist who served as director of the National Institute on Drug Abuse, and the National Institute of Mental Health, has held senior positions at the National Science Foundation and the American Association for the Advancement of Science, and serves on the National Science Board.
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.content:encoded:

item.title: Michael Baggot - Theories of Consciousness
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Get member exclusives like early access to new content with a free Closer To Truth account: https://closertotruth.com/
Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Fr. Michael Baggot is currently a Professor Aggregato of Bioethics at the Pontifical Athenaeum Regina Apostolorum in Rome, Italy. He also serves as a professor for the Joint Diploma in Leadership: Service through Virtues. He teaches at the Catholic Worldview Fellowship summer program in Germany and Italy. He was an Adjunct Professor of Theology at the Christendom College Rome program. In addition, Baggot is a Research Scholar at the UNESCO Chair in Bioethics and Human Rights. Baggot also serves as a member of the Scholarly Advisory Board for Magisterium AI.
Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://bsky.app/profile/closertotruth.bsky.social
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Terrence Sejnowski on ChatGPT and the Future of AI | Closer To Truth Chats
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls with a tax-deductible donation of any amount: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
In "ChatGPT and the Future of AI", Terry Sejnowski offers a nuanced exploration of large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT and what their future holds. How should we go about understanding LLMs? Do these language models truly understand what they are saying? Or is it possible that what appears to be intelligence in LLMs may be a mirror that merely reflects the intelligence of the interviewer? In his book, Sejnowski, a pioneer in computational approaches to understanding brain function, answers all our urgent questions about this astonishing new technology.
Terrence Joseph Sejnowski is the Francis Crick Professor at the Salk Institute for Biological Studies where he directs the Computational Neurobiology Laboratory and is the director of the Crick-Jacobs center for theoretical and computational biology.
Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

item.title: Judith Wolfe - Dualism: Arguing Pros and Cons
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Shop the Closer To Truth merch store for unique items like notebooks, mugs, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
Dualism is the theory that ultimate reality is composed of two radically different kinds of things, physical things and non-physical things, and that these same two kinds of things, expressed as body/brain and spirit/soul, account for consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Dualism (like the ‘interaction problem’)?
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Dr. Judith Wolfe is a Fellow in Theology at St John'sCollege in the University of Oxford. She has written widely on the interfacesbetween theology, philosophy and literature. She is General Editor of the Journal of Inklings Studies.
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Holmes Rolston III - What Kind of World did God Create?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Receive member exclusives like early access to new content by registering for a free Closer To Truth account: https://closertotruth.com/
To claim God is the Creator is to assume the burden of explaining why God selected and created this particular world. What are the characteristics of this world that could credential it as the one that the Supreme Being brought into existence? Theists do not like to argue that our world is the ‘best possible world’.
Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://bsky.app/profile/closertotruth.bsky.social
Holmes Rolston III was an American philosopher who was University Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at Colorado State University. He is best known for his contributions to environmental ethics and the relationship between science and religion.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

item.title: Timothy Pawl - Philosophy of the Bible
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4
The philosophy of fields applies analytical methods and tools of philosophy to areas of knowledge, from science and mathematics to law and aesthetics. What happens when philosophers who believe in the Bible try to construct a “philosophy of the Bible”?
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Timothy J. Pawl is Professor of Philosophy at the University of St. Thomas, Minneapolis. His work is in the fields of metaphysics, philosophical theology, and moral psychology. Pawl's books include "In Defense of Conciliar Christology: A Philosophical Essay" and "The Incarnation".

item.title: Philip Goff - The Field of Consciousness Studies
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Like Closer To Truth on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
What is the current state of the consciousness studies field? What are the major issues and trends? What kinds of experiments are being conducted? What kinds of arguments are being made?
Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/
Philip Goff is a British author, panpsychist philosopher, and professor at Durham University whose research focuses on philosophy of mind and consciousness. Specifically, it focuses on how consciousness can be part of the scientific worldview.

item.title: Joe Rogan Experience #2304 - Gary Brecka
item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:
Gary Brecka is a human biologist, biohacker, longevity expert, and host of the "Ultimate Human" podcast. https://www.theultimatehuman.com/ https://www.instagram.com/garybrecka/

item.title: Ernan McMullin - Is God the Cause of a Fine-Tuned Universe?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for new videos, announcements, and more: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/
Our universe is astounding in that the laws of physics have to be perfect beyond belief for stars and planets, and for human beings, to even exist. Does this demonstrate, if not prove, an intelligence behind the universe, who many call God? There are other, naturalistic explanations. Can we compare natural and supernatural reasons?
Register for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.
Ernan McMullin was an Irish philosopher who last served as the O’Hara Professor of Philosophy Emeritus at the University of Notre Dame. He was an internationally respected philosopher of science who has written and lectured extensively on subjects ranging from the relationship between cosmology and theology, to the role of values in understanding science, to the impact of Darwinism on Western religious thought. McMullin is the only person to ever hold the presidency of four of the major US philosophical associations.
Contribute what you can to support the show and help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq

item.author: PowerfulJRE
item.content:encoded:
Dave Smith is a stand-up comedian, libertarian political commentator, and podcaster. He's the host of the "Part of the Problem" podcast, as well as a co-host of the "Legion of Skanks” podcast. https://www.comicdavesmith.com
Douglas Murray is a political commentator, cultural critic, and author of numerous books, the most recent of which is "Democracies and Death Cults: Israel and the Future of Civilization." https://a.co/d/fUGXIZQ https://www.douglasmurray.net

item.title: Is AI morally neutral?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded: Dr. Mark Bailey argues that AI is inherently amoral, but can humans impose a different morality? Bailey's full interview coming soon. #shorts

item.title: Raymond Tallis - Can Neuroscience Alone Explain Consciousness?
item.author: Closer To Truth
item.content:encoded:
Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje
‘Neurophilosophy’ is the view that all that is mental or mind or consciousness can be solved ultimately by neuroscience. ‘Neuromania’ is the view that trying to explain consciousness by physical processes alone is impossible (if not ridiculous). The two views are diametrically opposed. Appreciate the arguments of each. Then decide, which is correct?
Like Closer To Truth on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l
Raymond C. Tallis is a philosopher, poet, novelist, cultural critic, and a retired medical physician and clinical neuroscientist.
Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq
Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.
